title: senecio scandens
intro: Senecio scandens, also known as creeping ragwort or climbing groundsel, is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a diuretic and to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is said to have health benefits including reducing inflammation and improving digestion, and has been traditionally used to treat conditions such as arthritis and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Senecio scandens is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used topically as a poultice or ointment.
